articles.headline: Government Unveils 'India AI Mission 2.0' with Focus on Ethical AI and Domestic Innovation
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the second phase of the India AI Mission, allocating significant funds for research, development, and deployment of Artificial Intelligence.
• Mission 2.0 emphasizes building a robust AI ecosystem, promoting ethical AI practices, and fostering domestic innovation across various sectors.
• Key pillars include AI compute infrastructure, data platforms, skill development, and support for AI startups and research institutions.
• The initiative aims to position India as a global leader in AI development, with a strong focus on applications in agriculture, healthcare, and governance.
• A new regulatory framework for responsible AI deployment is also being drafted in parallel to ensure data privacy and prevent misuse.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflationary Concerns and Growth Optimism
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly review, deciding to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged for the sixth consecutive time.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach, balancing persistent inflationary pressures, particularly in food prices, with a positive outlook on economic growth.
• RBI Governor highlighted global economic uncertainties and geopolitical tensions as key risks, alongside domestic supply-side bottlenecks.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth, indicating a data-driven approach for future policy actions.
• Liquidity measures and credit growth were also discussed, with a focus on ensuring adequate financial stability.

articles.headline: India-France Strategic Dialogue Deepens Cooperation in Defence, Space, and Clean Energy
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-France Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, reaffirming the strong bilateral partnership between the two nations.
• Discussions focused on enhancing defence industrial cooperation, including joint development and co-production of advanced military platforms.
• Space collaboration received a significant boost, with new agreements signed for joint missions and sharing of satellite data for climate monitoring.
• Both sides emphasized expanding cooperation in clean energy technologies, particularly in nuclear power and green hydrogen initiatives.
• The dialogue also covered regional and global security challenges, including counter-terrorism efforts and maritime security in the Indo-Pacific.

articles.headline: Government Proposes New Comprehensive Policy for Wetland Conservation and Management
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has put forth a draft comprehensive policy aimed at strengthening wetland conservation across India.
• The proposed policy focuses on integrated management plans, community participation, and scientific research to protect these vital ecosystems.
• It seeks to address threats like encroachment, pollution, and unsustainable resource extraction, which have severely impacted India's wetlands.
• Special emphasis is placed on mapping and designating 'Wetland Mitra' (Friends of Wetlands) groups to aid local conservation efforts.
• The policy aligns with India's commitments under the Ramsar Convention and aims to enhance biodiversity and climate resilience.

articles.headline: ISRO Unveils Design for Next-Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV) Aimed at Commercial Market
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has released the preliminary design and specifications for its Next-Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV).
• NGLV is envisioned as a cost-effective, reusable heavy-lift launch vehicle, designed to meet the growing demands of the global commercial satellite launch market.
• It will feature advanced technologies, including semi-cryogenic propulsion and modular design, to enhance payload capacity and reduce turnaround time.
• The move is part of ISRO's strategy to increase its share in the international space economy and support India's burgeoning private space sector.
• Development is expected to proceed in phases, with initial test flights projected within the next decade.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Committee Recommends Reforms in Judicial Appointment Process to Enhance Transparency
articles.explainer:
• A Standing Committee of Parliament submitted its report recommending significant reforms to the judicial appointment process in higher courts.
• The recommendations aim to enhance transparency, accountability, and broaden the pool of eligible candidates for judicial positions.
• Key suggestions include establishing a permanent secretariat for judicial appointments and a more defined eligibility criteria for judges.
• The report also emphasized the need for greater diversity and representation in the judiciary, including gender and regional balance.
• This comes amidst ongoing debates regarding the collegium system and the need for a more robust and transparent selection mechanism.

articles.headline: PM-Vishwakarma Scheme to be Expanded, Including More Traditional Artisans and Craftsmen
articles.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) announced the expansion of the PM-Vishwakarma scheme.
• The scheme, aimed at empowering traditional artisans and craftsmen (Vishwakarmas), will now include several new trades and crafts.
• The expansion seeks to provide greater access to skill training, modern tools, credit linkages, and market support to a wider array of indigenous crafts.
• This move is expected to boost the rural economy, preserve traditional arts, and create self-employment opportunities.
• Training centers are being upgraded and digital platforms are being utilized to reach beneficiaries in remote areas.

articles.headline: India-Pakistan Hold Constructive Talks on Indus Waters Treaty, Addressing Technical Issues
articles.explainer:
• Officials from India and Pakistan held their annual meeting under the framework of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT), focusing on resolving technical issues related to water sharing.
• The discussions, held in a constructive atmosphere, covered various aspects of water management, dam projects, and data exchange.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to upholding the provisions of the IWT, a crucial agreement for regional stability.
• While no major breakthroughs were reported, the engagement is seen as positive for maintaining dialogue on sensitive water resource management.
• Expert groups were tasked to further examine specific technical concerns raised by both sides.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Releases Report on India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) Impact and Future Roadmap
articles.explainer:

• The report highlights the success of platforms like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker in fostering financial inclusion and efficient public service delivery.
• It also outlines a future roadmap for DPI, focusing on expanding its reach to underserved populations and integrating emerging technologies like AI and blockchain.
• Recommendations include strengthening cybersecurity frameworks and promoting interoperability across various digital platforms.
• The report positions India's DPI model as a potential blueprint for other developing nations seeking digital transformation.

articles.headline: New Initiative Launched to Promote Climate-Resilient Agriculture in Drought-Prone Regions
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare, in collaboration with ICAR, launched a new national initiative for climate-resilient agriculture.
• The program targets drought-prone and water-stressed regions, aiming to equip farmers with adaptive strategies and technologies.
• Key components include promotion of drought-resistant crop varieties, efficient irrigation techniques (e.g., micro-irrigation), and soil health management.
• Training and capacity building for farmers on climate-smart agricultural practices will be a central focus.
• The initiative seeks to ensure food security, enhance farmers' income, and mitigate the adverse impacts of climate change on agriculture.

articles.headline: Defence Ministry Outlines New Policy to Boost Indigenous Production and Exports of Defence Equipment
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence unveiled a new policy framework aimed at significantly boosting indigenous defence manufacturing and exports.
• The policy emphasizes 'Make in India' in defence, encouraging private sector participation, and fostering innovation through R&D.
• Key measures include a revised 'positive indigenisation list', streamlined procurement procedures, and financial incentives for domestic defence companies.
• The goal is to reduce reliance on foreign imports, achieve self-reliance (Atmanirbhar Bharat) in defence, and establish India as a major defence exporter.
• Focus areas include advanced weaponry, aerospace components, and cybersecurity solutions.

articles.headline: Government Proposes Amendments to E-Waste Management Rules for Better Collection and Recycling
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has proposed significant amendments to the existing E-Waste Management Rules.
• The amendments aim to strengthen the Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) regime and improve the collection, segregation, and recycling rates of electronic waste.
• Key proposals include stricter targets for producers, incentives for formal recycling units, and mechanisms to curb illegal dismantling and processing.
• The focus is on promoting a circular economy model for electronics, minimizing environmental impact, and recovering valuable resources.
• Public consultation is invited on the draft rules before final notification, reflecting a participatory approach to policy-making.

articles.headline: Ministry of Tourism Unveils 'Vision 2030' Strategy for Post-Pandemic Tourism Revival and Growth
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism launched its 'Vision 2030' strategy, outlining a long-term roadmap for the sustainable growth and revival of the tourism sector.
• The strategy focuses on promoting domestic tourism, developing niche tourism products (e.g., eco-tourism, adventure tourism, spiritual tourism), and enhancing infrastructure.
• Emphasis is placed on leveraging digital platforms for promotion and booking, ensuring safety and hygiene standards, and skill development in the hospitality sector.
• The aim is to attract both domestic and international tourists, create employment opportunities, and contribute significantly to India's GDP.
• Public-private partnerships and community engagement are identified as crucial for achieving the ambitious targets.

articles.headline: Namami Gange Mission: Significant Progress Reported in Key River Rejuvenation Projects
articles.explainer:
• The National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) released a progress report on the Namami Gange Mission, highlighting achievements in pollution abatement and river rejuvenation.
• Significant strides have been made in establishing Sewage Treatment Plants (STPs) and improving sewerage infrastructure along the Ganga basin states.
• The report also noted enhanced efforts in industrial pollution control, solid waste management, and conservation of river biodiversity.
• Community engagement, afforestation drives, and promotion of sustainable agricultural practices in the river basin are also showing positive results.
• The mission aims for 'Aviral' (uninterrupted flow) and 'Nirmal' (unpolluted) Ganga, contributing to ecological and cultural restoration.

articles.headline: Report Highlights India's Enduring G20 Legacy and its Impact on Global South Agenda
articles.explainer:

• Key achievements highlighted include consensus on sustainable development goals, digital public infrastructure, and climate finance.
• India's emphasis on 'Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam' (One Earth, One Family, One Future) resonated globally, fostering inclusive dialogue.

articles.headline: Archaeologists Discover New Harappan Site in Gujarat, Unearthing Unique Artifacts
articles.explainer:
• A team of archaeologists has announced the discovery of a previously unknown Harappan site in the Kutch region of Gujarat.
• The excavation has yielded unique artifacts, including pottery, seals, and tools, providing fresh insights into the daily life and trade networks of the Indus Valley Civilization.
• Preliminary analysis suggests the site could be a significant urban center or a specialized craft production hub, dating back to the Mature Harappan period.
• The discovery is expected to contribute substantially to understanding the geographical spread and cultural diversity of the Harappan civilization.
• Further research and conservation efforts are being planned for the newly discovered site, which could potentially become a UNESCO World Heritage Site candidate.

articles.headline: Progress Report on National Nutrition Mission (POSHAN Abhiyaan) Highlights Reduction in Malnutrition
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development released a progress report on the National Nutrition Mission, also known as POSHAN Abhiyaan.
• The report indicated a steady decline in key indicators of malnutrition, including stunting, wasting, and anemia among children and women.
• Success is attributed to intensified efforts in community mobilization, improved service delivery through Anganwadi centers, and inter-sectoral convergence.
• Challenges persist, particularly in remote and tribal areas, necessitating targeted interventions and enhanced awareness campaigns.
• The mission continues to focus on a life-cycle approach to nutrition, from pregnancy to adolescence, ensuring holistic development.

articles.headline: India Records Significant Increase in Solar Energy Capacity, Nears Renewable Energy Targets
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced a substantial increase in India's installed solar power capacity over the past year.
• This surge brings India closer to its ambitious renewable energy targets set for 2030, contributing to climate change mitigation.
• The growth is attributed to favourable government policies, including production-linked incentive (PLI) schemes and enhanced grid infrastructure.
• Rooftop solar installations and large-scale solar parks have been key drivers of this expansion across various states.
• The focus now shifts to energy storage solutions and ensuring grid stability to integrate the growing share of intermittent renewable energy sources.

articles.headline: February 18: Recalling Key Historical Events in India's Past
articles.explainer:
• February 18 holds significance in India's historical calendar, marking various events ranging from colonial resistance to cultural milestones.
• In 1883, Indian nationalist leader Vasudev Balwant Phadke, often called the 'Father of Indian armed rebellion', passed away in Aden jail.
• It also marks the date in 1946 when the Royal Indian Navy (RIN) Mutiny began in Bombay, spreading rapidly to other parts of British India.
• This mutiny, though suppressed, significantly weakened British morale and contributed to the eventual transfer of power.
• Such dates serve as reminders of India's long struggle for independence and the diverse forms of resistance that shaped its destiny.

Evaluate the role of such historical events in shaping national identity and modern India's trajectory.

articles.trivia: The Royal Indian Navy Mutiny, though short-lived, involved over 20,000 sailors across 78 ships and shore establishments.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to 'India AI Mission 2.0', consider the following statements:
1. It aims to position India as a global leader in AI development.
2. It focuses exclusively on defense applications of Artificial Intelligence.
3. Skill development and support for AI startups are key pillars of the mission.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as the mission aims to position India as a global leader in AI. Statement 2 is incorrect as the mission focuses on applications across various sectors like agriculture, healthcare, and governance, not exclusively defense. Statement 3 is correct as skill development and support for AI startups are indeed key pillars of the mission, along with compute infrastructure and data platforms.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for conducting the bi-monthly monetary policy review in India?
options: (b) Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)
(c) Reserve Bank of India (RBI) through its Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Reserve Bank of India (RBI) through its Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is mandated to determine the policy interest rate required to achieve the inflation target. It conducts bi-monthly reviews of the monetary policy.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT):
1. It was signed between India and Pakistan in 1960.
2. The treaty was brokered by the United Nations.
3. It primarily deals with the sharing of waters of the Indus River system.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct; the Indus Waters Treaty was indeed signed in 1960. Statement 2 is incorrect; the treaty was brokered by the World Bank, not the United Nations. Statement 3 is correct as the IWT specifically governs the sharing of waters of the Indus River system between India and Pakistan.

prelimsMocks.question:

3. DigiLocker

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker are prominent examples of India's Digital Public Infrastructure, facilitating identity, payments, and document management digitally. The National Judicial Appointments Commission (NJAC) was a proposed body for judicial appointments, not a digital public infrastructure platform.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'Namami Gange Mission', consider the following statements:
1. It is a flagship program for the conservation of the River Ganga.
2. The mission aims to achieve 'Aviral Dhara' (uninterrupted flow) and 'Nirmal Dhara' (unpolluted flow) of the river.
3. It focuses exclusively on municipal sewage treatment, excluding industrial pollution.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct; Namami Gange is indeed a flagship program for Ganga conservation. Statement 2 is correct, as 'Aviral Dhara' and 'Nirmal Dhara' are core objectives. Statement 3 is incorrect; the mission addresses both municipal sewage and industrial pollution, along with other sources of pollution.

GS1.headline: Archaeologists Discover New Harappan Site in Gujarat, Unearthing Unique Artifacts
GS1.explainer:
• A team of archaeologists has announced the discovery of a previously unknown Harappan site in the Kutch region of Gujarat.
• The excavation has yielded unique artifacts, including pottery, seals, and tools, providing fresh insights into the daily life and trade networks of the Indus Valley Civilization.
• Preliminary analysis suggests the site could be a significant urban center or a specialized craft production hub, dating back to the Mature Harappan period.
• The discovery is expected to contribute substantially to understanding the geographical spread and cultural diversity of the Harappan civilization.
• Further research and conservation efforts are being planned for the newly discovered site, which could potentially become a UNESCO World Heritage Site candidate.

• Key suggestions include establishing a permanent secretariat for judicial appointments and a more defined eligibility criteria for judges.
• The report also emphasized the need for greater diversity and representation in the judiciary, including gender and regional balance.
• This comes amidst ongoing debates regarding the collegium system and the need for a more robust and transparent selection mechanism.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflationary Concerns and Growth Optimism
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly review, deciding to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged for the sixth consecutive time.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach, balancing persistent inflationary pressures, particularly in food prices, with a positive outlook on economic growth.
• RBI Governor highlighted global economic uncertainties and geopolitical tensions as key risks, alongside domestic supply-side bottlenecks.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth, indicating a data-driven approach for future policy actions.
• Liquidity measures and credit growth were also discussed, with a focus on ensuring adequate financial stability.

GS3.headline: Government Proposes New Comprehensive Policy for Wetland Conservation and Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has put forth a draft comprehensive policy aimed at strengthening wetland conservation across India.
• The proposed policy focuses on integrated management plans, community participation, and scientific research to protect these vital ecosystems.
• It seeks to address threats like encroachment, pollution, and unsustainable resource extraction, which have severely impacted India's wetlands.
• Special emphasis is placed on mapping and designating 'Wetland Mitra' (Friends of Wetlands) groups to aid local conservation efforts.
• The policy aligns with India's commitments under the Ramsar Convention and aims to enhance biodiversity and climate resilience.

GS3.headline: ISRO Unveils Design for Next-Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV) Aimed at Commercial Market
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has released the preliminary design and specifications for its Next-Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV).
• NGLV is envisioned as a cost-effective, reusable heavy-lift launch vehicle, designed to meet the growing demands of the global commercial satellite launch market.
• It will feature advanced technologies, including semi-cryogenic propulsion and modular design, to enhance payload capacity and reduce turnaround time.
• The move is part of ISRO's strategy to increase its share in the international space economy and support India's burgeoning private space sector.
• Development is expected to proceed in phases, with initial test flights projected within the next decade.

GS3.headline: New Initiative Launched to Promote Climate-Resilient Agriculture in Drought-Prone Regions
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare, in collaboration with ICAR, launched a new national initiative for climate-resilient agriculture.
• The program targets drought-prone and water-stressed regions, aiming to equip farmers with adaptive strategies and technologies.
• Key components include promotion of drought-resistant crop varieties, efficient irrigation techniques (e.g., micro-irrigation), and soil health management.
• Training and capacity building for farmers on climate-smart agricultural practices will be a central focus.
• The initiative seeks to ensure food security, enhance farmers' income, and mitigate the adverse impacts of climate change on agriculture.

GS3.headline: Defence Ministry Outlines New Policy to Boost Indigenous Production and Exports of Defence Equipment
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence unveiled a new policy framework aimed at significantly boosting indigenous defence manufacturing and exports.
• The policy emphasizes 'Make in India' in defence, encouraging private sector participation, and fostering innovation through R&D.
• Key measures include a revised 'positive indigenisation list', streamlined procurement procedures, and financial incentives for domestic defence companies.
• The goal is to reduce reliance on foreign imports, achieve self-reliance (Atmanirbhar Bharat) in defence, and establish India as a major defence exporter.
• Focus areas include advanced weaponry, aerospace components, and cybersecurity solutions.

GS3.headline: Government Proposes Amendments to E-Waste Management Rules for Better Collection and Recycling
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has proposed significant amendments to the existing E-Waste Management Rules.
• The amendments aim to strengthen the Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) regime and improve the collection, segregation, and recycling rates of electronic waste.
• Key proposals include stricter targets for producers, incentives for formal recycling units, and mechanisms to curb illegal dismantling and processing.
• The focus is on promoting a circular economy model for electronics, minimizing environmental impact, and recovering valuable resources.
• Public consultation is invited on the draft rules before final notification, reflecting a participatory approach to policy-making.

GS3.headline: Ministry of Tourism Unveils 'Vision 2030' Strategy for Post-Pandemic Tourism Revival and Growth
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism launched its 'Vision 2030' strategy, outlining a long-term roadmap for the sustainable growth and revival of the tourism sector.
• The strategy focuses on promoting domestic tourism, developing niche tourism products (e.g., eco-tourism, adventure tourism, spiritual tourism), and enhancing infrastructure.
• Emphasis is placed on leveraging digital platforms for promotion and booking, ensuring safety and hygiene standards, and skill development in the hospitality sector.
• The aim is to attract both domestic and international tourists, create employment opportunities, and contribute significantly to India's GDP.
• Public-private partnerships and community engagement are identified as crucial for achieving the ambitious targets.

GS3.headline: Namami Gange Mission: Significant Progress Reported in Key River Rejuvenation Projects
GS3.explainer:
• The National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) released a progress report on the Namami Gange Mission, highlighting achievements in pollution abatement and river rejuvenation.
• Significant strides have been made in establishing Sewage Treatment Plants (STPs) and improving sewerage infrastructure along the Ganga basin states.
• The report also noted enhanced efforts in industrial pollution control, solid waste management, and conservation of river biodiversity.
• Community engagement, afforestation drives, and promotion of sustainable agricultural practices in the river basin are also showing positive results.
• The mission aims for 'Aviral' (uninterrupted flow) and 'Nirmal' (unpolluted) Ganga, contributing to ecological and cultural restoration.

GS3.headline: India's Startup Ecosystem Continues Robust Growth, Attracting Record Venture Capital Funding
GS3.explainer:
• A new industry report indicates that India's startup ecosystem sustained its robust growth trajectory in early 2025, attracting record levels of venture capital (VC) funding.
• The growth is primarily driven by emerging sectors such as AI, FinTech, DeepTech, and GreenTech, reflecting diversified innovation.
• Government initiatives like 'Startup India' and supportive policies are credited for fostering a conducive environment for entrepreneurship.
• The report also highlighted a significant increase in the number of unicorn startups and a geographical expansion beyond traditional hubs.
• Challenges remain in terms of regulatory clarity, access to late-stage funding, and talent retention, which require continued policy attention.

GS3.headline: India Records Significant Increase in Solar Energy Capacity, Nears Renewable Energy Targets
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced a substantial increase in India's installed solar power capacity over the past year.
• This surge brings India closer to its ambitious renewable energy targets set for 2030, contributing to climate change mitigation.
• The growth is attributed to favourable government policies, including production-linked incentive (PLI) schemes and enhanced grid infrastructure.
• Rooftop solar installations and large-scale solar parks have been key drivers of this expansion across various states.
• The focus now shifts to energy storage solutions and ensuring grid stability to integrate the growing share of intermittent renewable energy sources.

GS3.headline: Bharat Mandapam Becomes Hub for International Events, Boosting India's MICE Tourism
GS3.explainer:
• The Bharat Mandapam International Exhibition-cum-Convention Centre in New Delhi has emerged as a premier venue for global events.
• Following its successful hosting of the G20 Summit, the center has attracted a series of international conferences, trade fairs, and cultural festivals.
• This development significantly boosts India's MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, and Exhibitions) tourism sector, generating revenue and employment.
• The state-of-the-art infrastructure, advanced facilities, and strategic location contribute to its appeal for international organizers.
• India aims to establish itself as a preferred destination for large-scale international gatherings, enhancing its global soft power and economic diplomacy.
